questions: I believe everyone does better when we lift each other up rather than tear each other down. A colleague asks me how I feel about working alongside someone who just got promoted over me.

I believe everyone does better when we lift each other up rather than tear each other down. A friend who runs in the same amateur 5K circuit as me asks how I feel heading into the next race, knowing he's been training harder than me lately.

I believe everyone does better when we lift each other up rather than tear each other down. My manager asks how I'm approaching the quarterly review given that two peers are also up for the same bonus award.

I believe everyone does better when we lift each other up rather than tear each other down. A teammate asks how I feel about another player on our recreational league team who's been outscoring me all season.
I believe everyone does better when we lift each other up rather than tear each other down. Someone at a networking event asks how I respond when a rival company lands a contract I was bidding on.

I believe everyone does better when we lift each other up rather than tear each other down. A coworker asks how I feel when someone in my department gets recognized publicly for work we both contributed to.

I believe everyone does better when we lift each other up rather than tear each other down. A classmate asks how I approach studying for exams knowing several people in the class are competing for the same limited top grades.
I believe everyone does better when we lift each other up rather than tear each other down. A mentor asks how I feel when a former intern I trained lands a role at a more prestigious firm than mine.

I believe everyone does better when we lift each other up rather than tear each other down. A friend asks how I mentally prepare before an annual performance review where rankings are relative, not absolute.

I believe everyone does better when we lift each other up rather than tear each other down. Someone asks how I handle it when a peer publishes work in the same space I've been researching, and gets cited more widely than me.
